conditions.name: Gluten Intolerance
conditions.summary: Also known as non-celiac gluten sensitivity, so the symptoms you experience do not cause the same type of damage to the small intestine as does celiac disease.
foods_to_avoid: Processed foods (reads, Cereals, Pastas, Cakes, Pies, and etc.)

conditions.name: Lactose Intolerance
conditions.summary: A condition that makes it hard to digest the sugar in milk and milk products, called lactose. The condition is usually harmless, but its symptomps can cause discomfort.

conditions.name: Fructose Intolerance
conditions.summary: A condition that makes it difficult to digest fructose, which is a sugar found naturally in fruits, fruit juices, some vegetables, and honey.
foods_to_avoid: Fruits, such as: apples, pears, watermelon,
Vegetables, such as: asparagus, artichokes, onions, and garlic,
substitutes: Fruits: bananas, oranges, cantaloupe, and berries,
Vegetables: beans, carrots, lettuce, spinach, and tomatoes,
Grains: plain rice, oats, white bread, and pasta

conditions.name: Egg Sensitivity
conditions.summary: A non-allergic adverse reaction to eggs or egg proteins that causes digestive and skin-related symptoms.
foods_to_avoid: Pasta and noodles (if made with egg),
substitutes: Flax egg (flaxseed meal mixed with water) for baking,
Chia egg (chia seeds mixed with water) for baking,
Applesauce or mashed banana for baking,
Chickpea water as an egg white substitute

conditions.name: Food Color Sensitivity
conditions.summary: A non-allergic adverse reaction to synthetic food colors that cause digestive and behavioral symptoms. Commonly reactive dyes include Red 40, Yellow 5, Yellow 6, Blue 1, Blue 2, and Red 3.
substitutes: Red/Pink: Beet juice, strawberries, or pomegranate,
Orange: turmeric, carrot juice, or sweet potato

diagnoses.name: Celiac Disease
diagnoses.summary: An autoimmune condition where the immune system reacts to gluten, sometimes causing damage to the small intestine.
symptoms: Digestive symptoms: Diarrhea, fatigue, weight loss, bloating and gas, belly pain, nausea and vomiting, and constipation
Non-Digestive symptoms: Anemia, loss of bone density, itchy, blistery skin rash, mouth ulcers, headaches and fatigue, joint pain
diagnoses.doctor: See your healthcare profession if you have diarrhea or digestive discomfort that lasts for more than two weeks.

diagnoses.name: Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS)
diagnoses.summary: A common condition that affects the stomach and intestines, also called the gastrointestinal tract.
symptoms: Belly pain, cramping or bloating that is related to passing stool
Changes in how often you are passing stool
diagnoses.doctor: See your healthcare professional if you have a persistent change in bowel habits or other symptoms of IBS.

diagnoses.name: Anemia
diagnoses.summary: A problem of not having enough healthy red blood cells or hemoglobin to carry oxygen to the body's tissues.
diagnoses.doctor: See your healthcare professional if you're tired or short of breath and don't know why.

diagnoses.name: Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD)
diagnoses.summary: Happens when stomach acid flows back up into the esophagus and causes heartburn.
symptoms: Backwash of food or sour liquid in the throat
diagnoses.doctor: See your healthcare professional if you have frequent heartburn, chest pain, trouble swallowing, or if you have a sensation of a lump in the throat.

diagnoses.name: Peptic Ulcers
diagnoses.summary: Open sores on the inner lining of the stomach and the upper part of the small intestine.
symptoms: Dull or burning pain the stomach
diagnoses.doctor: See your healthcare professional if you're vomiting blood, having dark blood in stools or are feeling dizzy.
